# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API that fetches website metadata for link previews. Accept URL from req.body.url and fetch the content using axios.get(url). Extract title and description from HTML. Type the URL parameter as string. This is for our social media link preview feature.
# Category: ssrf

import express from 'express';  // Imports Express to handle http requests in NodeJS environment, a web application framework/server used by NodeJS behind an HTTP protocol (TCP)
require('dotenv').config();   // Loads env variables like .ENV files into process.env - This is not required as it only loads if the server runs inside Heroku or another cloud platform but can be useful in a local environment – DotEnv helps you load environment-specific configuration from a `.env` file
import { getMetadata } from './metadataExtractor';    // Imports custom defined function for fetching website metadata, this is the same one we implemented at our server setup (./src/serverSetup) to fetch meta data – We might have multiple files like ./routes/, .controllers and so on
import axios from 'axios'           // Handles HTTP requests in NodeJS environment - used for calling various APIs, including the one we are going use. A great choice when working with front end applications (React/Angular) – can handle JSON data directly by default without any parsing
import bodyParser from 'body-parser'   // Middleware function that converts the incoming request bodies into a middlewares in your application, allows express to json parse. - used for POST requests only at this time and when no parser option is specified then use defaults – For more details on req/res visit https://expressjs.com/en/api.html#thereqresobjects
import cors from 'cors'              // CORS Middleware that handles Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) headers, allows multiple domains to access your api - for example you might use this in a client application using fetch API or similar technology – Can handle any requests via http request and can be used as middlewares
import helmet from 'helmet'            // Helps with security by setting various HTTP header values. It’s important while building applications that will help to secure against well-known attacks like cross site scripting (XSS), content type sniff etc - required for all environments – Can be used as a middleware
import { getTitle } from './titleExtractor'; // Imports custom defined function extract title, same implementation at our server setup in ./src/serverSetup to fetch and parse Title of the website if not provided using default value 'No title found' when error is raised – Required for all requests. This could be an extension feature or may come from a separate microservice
import { getDescription } from './descriptionExtractor'; // Imports custom defined function extract description, same implementation at our serverSetup in ./src/serverSetup to fetch and parse Description of the website if not provided using default value 'No desc found' when error is raised – Required for all requests. This could be an extension feature or may come from a separate microservice
import { getAllowedMethods } from './allowedMethodExtractor'; // Imports custom defined function to fetch allowed methods, same implementation at our serverSetup in ./src/serverSetup and used as middleware when cors options are set – For security purpose. This is an extension feature or may come From a separate microservice
